from trading.paper_trading import PaperBook, auto_enter_from_signals
from trading.portfolio_risk import RiskLimits, size_position


def test_uppercase_short_without_stop_gets_side_correct_default():
    book = PaperBook()
    entered = auto_enter_from_signals(
        book,
        [{
            "ticker": "ORCL",
            "entry_price": 80.0,
            "target_price": 75.0,
            "confidence": 0.91,
            "direction": " SHORT ",
        }],
        20_000.0,
    )
    assert entered == 1
    trade = book.trades[0]
    assert trade.direction == "short"
    assert trade.stop_price == 82.4
    assert book.emitted[0].direction == "short"


def test_invalid_short_stop_is_not_rescued_by_quantity_fallback():
    book = PaperBook()
    entered = auto_enter_from_signals(
        book,
        [{
            "ticker": "NOW",
            "entry_price": 100.0,
            "stop_price": 96.0,
            "target_price": 92.0,
            "confidence": 0.88,
            "direction": "short",
        }],
        10_000.0,
    )
    assert entered == 0
    assert book.trades == []


def test_unknown_direction_remains_long_and_long_behavior_stays_stable():
    book = PaperBook()
    entered = auto_enter_from_signals(
        book,
        [{
            "ticker": "IBM",
            "entry_price": 100.0,
            "stop_price": 95.0,
            "target_price": 107.0,
            "confidence": 0.80,
            "direction": "sideways",
        }],
        10_000.0,
    )
    assert entered == 1
    assert book.trades[0].direction == "long"
    assert book.emitted[0].direction == "long"
    assert size_position(10_000.0, 100.0, 95.0, limits=RiskLimits(), direction="sideways") == 20
